summary: Comprehensive pytest regression suite for Vibe2Nite backend. 19/19 tests pass. Verified health, /vibes/top (best/live/gem ordering, live_music fallback, out-of-range null, lat/lng 422 validation), /feedback (busy/dead/good deltas, user_votes clamp 0-10, invalid vote 422, unknown venue 404, persistence via /admin), /admin/venues (list shape, POST create 201, PATCH signals recomputes score+crowd, 404 on unknown id), scoring math (weighted sum within 0.01 + cap at 10 for all 12 seeded venues + crowd bands >=8 busy/>=5 medium/<5 dead), branded docs (references /api/static/vibe2nite.css and /api/openapi.json), and openapi schema contains all required paths.

minor.issue: No delete endpoint exists, so test-created TEST_ venues accumulate in the DB. One TEST_The Vibe Lab venue left behind after this run (id 1d79db9a-8112-473a-aba3-baae12a97306). Consider adding DELETE for cleanup or a soft-delete flag.
No admin auth yet (noted as 'future auth' in code). Anyone can create venues and update signals. Fine for MVP but must be secured before production.

No rate limiting / per-user identity — a single client can spam votes and push user_votes to cap 10 instantly (confirmed in test_create_and_update_signals_recomputes_score which did this with 10 busy votes).

action_items: Optional: add DELETE /api/admin/venues/{id} for test/teardown cleanup.
Optional: add admin auth and per-user/ip rate limiting on /api/feedback before production.
critical_code_review_comments: scoring.py weights sum to 1.0 and are correctly applied; compute_vibe_score clamps 0-10 and rounds to 2dp — matches spec exactly.
recommendations.get_top_vibes correctly implements live_music fallback: when no live_music venue is in radius, it reuses `best` (best_overall) — verified.
feedback_service.apply_feedback clamps user_votes in [0,10] and recomputes vibe_score + crowd_level inside the same transaction — correct.

admin.create_venue: Base.metadata.create_all runs on app boot AND migrations exist — acceptable belt-and-suspenders, but should be removed once alembic is the only source of truth to avoid schema drift.
main.py enables CORS with allow_origins=['*'] AND allow_credentials=True — this combination is rejected by browsers and is a security smell. Restrict origins before prod.
No DELETE endpoint for venues => test data accumulates; add one or expose prefix-based cleanup.
Admin endpoints have zero auth (explicitly acknowledged in file docstring as 'future auth to be added'). Must be guarded before any public deploy.
seed_data_creation: Test created one venue named 'TEST_The Vibe Lab' (category=club, lat=40.731, lng=-73.991) via POST /api/admin/venues. It remains in the DB because no DELETE endpoint exists.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 19 pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py pass against supervisor-hosted backend at http://localhost:8001. Tests are idempotent except for the single TEST_ venue creation. Re-run with: `cd /app/backend && pytest tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. No frontend in this repo (no /app/frontend/.env), so REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL falls back to localhost:8001.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. All 13 feature/behavior items from the review request verified passing.
